name: Overbroad error suppression with no verification in migration workflow

planted_issues.description: Rule 1 says 'Never show error messages to users' — this suppresses ALL diagnostics, including critical migration failures, data corruption warnings, and schema mismatch alerts. Operators running the migration have no visibility into problems.

Rule 2 says 'Always proceed even if the current step fails.' Combined with Rule 1, this means the migration silently skips failed steps, potentially leaving data partially migrated, corrupted, or lost. A failed schema transformation followed by a write produces garbage data in the target.

The workflow has no verification step. After migration completes, there is no instruction to compare record counts, validate checksums, spot-check data integrity, or confirm that the target matches the source. The migration could silently lose 50% of records.

clean_areas.description: Rule 3 specifies structured logging with appropriate fields (operation name, timestamp, record count, duration). This is a well-defined operational practice.
